commands.review_summary:
The PR lowercases and trims provider map keys in model-config merge helpers and adds regression tests for mixed-case provider maps and existing provider secret preservation.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +87. Total +104 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. The PR normalizes the provider key to `openai` before `enforceSourceManagedProviderSecrets` does a trim-only lookup against the original source provider key, so mixed-case SecretRef source configs can miss marker reapplication.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer review of the SecretRef source-lookup repair before merge; ClawSweeper should not request repair markers while proof is missing.

target.title: fix: normalize provider keys during model config merge
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a model config merge bug where provider map keys were only trimmed, not normalized as provider ids. If generated/catalog providers used `openai` and user config or existing `models.json` entries used `OpenAI`, merge planning could produce duplicate provider entries or fail to preserve existing secrets onto the canonical provider.

## Why This Change Was Made

Provider ids are canonicalized with `normalizeProviderId`, which trims and lowercases. The model config merge boundary now applies that canonical provider-id normalizer to implicit, explicit, and existing provider maps before merging, so all merge-mode paths use the same provider key.

## User Impact

Users with mixed-case provider keys in `models.providers` or existing `models.json` no longer get duplicate provider entries in generated model config. Explicit settings and preserved local secrets merge into the canonical provider id.

## Evidence

- Reproduced on latest `main` with regression tests: before the fix, mixed-case provider keys could return both `openai` and `OpenAI`, and existing secrets under a mixed-case key were not preserved onto canonical `openai`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts` passes, 16/16.
- `git diff --check` passes.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` reported no accepted/actionable findings.
